key_insight: Augmenting graph neural network atom features with site-projected density of states from DFT captures local electronic structure and significantly improves materials property prediction.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to build more accurate property predictors for materials discovery by combining first-principles electronic information with crystal-graph learning.
method: Compute orbital-projected density of states (pDOS) fingerprints for each atomic site from DFT.
Fuse pDOS features with Pettifor elemental embeddings before message passing in a graph neural network.
Use a spectral attention-gating mechanism to identify which orbitals and energy ranges matter most for each target property.
evidence_signal: Compared with an elemental-descriptor baseline, pDOS augmentation reduced errors by 22.9% for superconducting Tc and 27.9% for optical dielectric constant ε∞, with gains comparable to doubling the training set.
watch_item: The benefit depends on dataset size and is weaker when the training set is smaller than the pDOS feature dimension, so the added electronic descriptors may not help equally in data-scarce cases.
paper_title: Machine Learning Materials Properties by Encoding Orbital-Projected Density of States
